data.title: The top Quant restaurant stocks as the sector has rebounded from a year ago
data.description:
Although real restaurant spending declined -5.7% month-over-month in June, it has rebounded sharply on an annual basis.

According to the U.S. Census Bureau, advance estimates of U.S. retail and food services sales for June 2025 were more than $720B, up 0.6% month-over-month, and up 3.9% from last year.

Food service and drinking places were up 6.6% from June of last year.

 

 

 

“Overall, the consumer has not thrown in the towel, and we'll need to watch what happens on the services side of the economy, but certainly the report wasn't as rosy as the headlines suggest,” said Parker Ross, global chief economist at Arch Capital Group, via X (formerly Twitter).

Here are the 10 top-rated stocks by Seeking Alpha’s Quant metrics, and their performances from June 2024:

  1. BJ’s Restaurants (BJRI) – Up 4.1% from a year ago; Quant rating: 4.38 (Buy)
  2. Potbelly Corp. (PBPB) – Up 67.2% from a year ago; Quant rating: 3.55 (Buy)
  3. Serve Robotics (SERV) – Up 409.66% from a year ago; Quant rating: 3.48 (Hold)
  4. Cracker Barrel Old Country (CBRL) – Up 41.5% from a year ago; Quant rating: 3.39 (Hold)
  5. Papa John’s International (PZZA) – Down 0.9% from a year ago; Quant rating: 2.88 (Hold)
  6. Dine Brands Global (DIN) – Down 37.1% from a year ago; Quant rating: 2.75 (Hold)
  7. Super Hi International (HDL) – Up 13.2% from a year ago; Quant rating: 2.69 (Hold)
  8. Portillo’s (PTLO) – Up 12.8% from a year ago; Quant rating: 2.63 (Hold)
  9. Bloomin’ Brands (BLMN) – Down 52.5% from a year ago; Quant rating: 2.63 (Hold)
  10. Arcos Dorados (ARCO) – Down 24.5% from a year ago; Quant rating: 2.62 (Hold)

Seeking Alpha's Quant system consistently outperforms the S&P 500 (SP500), driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics.

data.title: 3M Q2 Preview: What to expect
data.description:
3M (NYSE:MMM) is scheduled to report Q2 results on Thursday, July 17th, after market close, with analysts expecting both earnings and revenue to see a YY jump.

The Post-it maker’s EPS is projected to see a 4.1% year-on-year jump to $2.01, while revenue is also likely to come at $6.07B, representing a 1.2% jump compared to the year-ago quarter.

Last quarter, the company's shares jumped after it posted better-than-expected results. While the company is expected to record solid growth this quarter, investors are also likely to look out for management's comments on tariff-related impact.

Last quarter, CEO William Brown had warned that tariffs may have a negative effect on profits for the year.

“Tariffs are going to be a headwind this year, but we thought it would be prudent to hold the impact outside of our full-year guidance, while I digest the new policies and fully develop and quantify mitigation plans,” Brown had said.

The company has priced in tariff-related headwinds of $850 million annually, with anticipated mitigation actions reducing the impact by $0.20 to $0.40 on EPS.

Discussing early Q2 trends and potential pre-buying, CEO Brown had then noted limited pre-buy activity, highlighted ongoing order strength in the industrial segment, while pointing to weakness in the transportation and electronics sectors.

Over the last 2 years, 3M has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 63% of the time. Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 1 upward revision and 11 downward moves while revenue estimates have seen 7 upward revisions and 3 downward adjustments.

On the year-to-date basis, the company has gone up by 21.66% compared to about a 7% rise in the broader markets.

data.title: OFG Bancorp raises 2025 loan growth outlook to 5–6% while launching $100M share buyback
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: OFG Bancorp (OFG) Q2 2025

Management View

  • José Rafael Fernández, President, CEO & Chairman, announced "record assets of more than $12 billion and record loans of more than $8 billion." He highlighted "excellent financial results, generating earnings per share diluted of $1.15 for a 6.5% increase year-over-year on a 1.5% increase in total core revenue" and a "new $100 million stock buyback authorization and bought back more shares supported by our strong capital generation and balance sheet."
  • Fernández emphasized ongoing momentum in digital transformation, stating "we continue to see strong momentum with our omnichannel digital platform, our strategic investments in technology and innovation through our digital first strategy is paying off."
  • He introduced two new products: "Oriental Marketplace, an online feature that gives our customers exclusive discounts..." and "a U.S. government money market fund, a new addition to our DGI family of funds."
  • Maritza Arizmendi Diaz, CFO, reported, "Core revenues totaled $182 million. Looking at the key components. Total interest income was $194 million, an increase of $5 million... Total banking and financial service revenues were $30 million, an increase of $1 million."
  • She highlighted, "noninterest expenses... totaled $94.8 million, up $1.4 million. This is in line with our continued outlook of $95 million to $96 million in quarterly noninterest expenses in 2025."
  • CFO Arizmendi Diaz added, "Tangible book value was $27.67 per share. During the quarter, we bought back 186,000 shares. Efficiency ratio was 52%. Return on average asset was 1.73% and return on average tangible common equity was 17%."

Outlook

  • Fernández indicated continued loan growth, stating, "loan balances to grow for the full year 2025, now closer to the 5%, 6% versus the 3%, 4% that we previously guided."
  • Management expects "overall retail customer deposits will continue to grow in the second half. We expect that to continue and also into 2026."
  • CFO Arizmendi Diaz maintained expense guidance: "continued outlook of $95 million to $96 million in quarterly noninterest expenses in 2025."
  • Tax rate guidance for the year was provided as "24.9% for the year and it doesn't have any discrete items included within that."

Financial Results

  • OFG Bancorp reported "core revenues totaled $182 million... Total interest income was $194 million, an increase of $5 million... Total banking and financial service revenues were $30 million, an increase of $1 million."
  • Noninterest expenses were stated as "$94.8 million, up $1.4 million."
  • Loan origination reached "$784 million, up 38% from the first quarter and 33% from a year ago."
  • End-of-period loans held for investment totaled "$8.2 billion, up 7% from a year ago and up $328 million from the last quarter."
  • Net interest margin was reported as "5.31% compared to 5.42%."
  • Net charge-offs totaled "$13 million, down $7.6 million from the first quarter. Net charge-off rate was 0.64%, down 41 basis points sequentially."
  • CET1 ratio was "13.99%... tangible common equity ratio decreased 10 basis points to 10.20%."

Q&A

  • Timur Felixovich Braziler, Wells Fargo: Asked about margin expansion and deposit costs. Fernández responded, "government deposits are tied to variable rate treasury bill kind of formula... That's why you're seeing a little bit of that uptick on the cost of deposits... overall retail customer deposits will continue to grow in the second half."
  • Braziler inquired about the consistency of loan growth. Fernández explained, "what drove it, it was primarily a very strong pipeline coming into the second quarter in both Puerto Rico and the U.S... we also saw an increase in commercial line utilization from some larger commercial clients."
  • Kelly Ann Motta, KBW: Probed on margin expectations and liquidity. CFO Arizmendi Diaz clarified, "the driver was a volume factor for the NII to continue expanding... As we continue to see loan growth... we decided to accelerate and put funding to the books with a good rate."
  • Motta asked about loan spreads and business sources. Fernández said, "the market is much more competitive on the lending side... we're seeing a little bit more pricing pressure... mortgage... we've done $22 million more than the first half of last year."
  • Brett D. Rabatin, Hovde Group: Sought color on Puerto Rico's power grid. Fernández responded, "it's going to be noisy. But at the end, it's not making any impact on the economy. It's having an effect... but it's not... the economy is in such strong base right now..."
  • Rabatin queried about credit quality. Ortiz-Marcano, CRO, explained, "first quarter always benefits in delinquencies and nonperforming loan statistics because it a seasonal positive quarter... it's a new vintage coming in, better vintages coming in that we adjusted back in 2022."
  • Motta asked about government deposits' stability. Fernández noted, "the expectation is relatively the same as in past quarters... we're going to see this deposit kind of roll over in several more quarters."
  • Motta probed on expense investments and balancing tech with capital returns. Fernández answered, "technology is helping out... we have not yet seen some of those efficiencies, but we are expecting to see them in the years to come in the next year or so."
  • Motta sought clarification on tax guidance. Arizmendi Diaz confirmed, "24.9% for the year and it doesn't have any discrete items included."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly positive, frequently commending strong loan growth and operational execution, but pressing on deposit costs, loan spreads, and strategic investments; "Great quarter" was expressed by Motta.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone during both prepared remarks and Q&A, with Fernández stating, "we continue to execute our plan strategically and thoughtfully, growing market share by creating value..."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts expressed increased optimism, with management raising loan growth guidance and analysts recognizing execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Loan growth guidance increased from "3%, 4%" to "5%, 6%" for 2025.
  • Loan origination jumped from $559 million to $784 million quarter-over-quarter.
  • End-of-period loans held for investment rose by $328 million compared to $61 million last quarter.
  • Net charge-offs decreased significantly from $20 million to $13 million.
  • Noninterest expenses remained in line with guidance.
  • Management's tone shifted to greater confidence, supported by record asset levels and increased shareholder returns through the new buyback program.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from deposit growth and digital channel usage to loan growth, margin sustainability, and competitive dynamics.

Risks and Concerns

  • Fernández identified competitive pressures in both deposits and lending, particularly from "a small commercial bank that has operations in Florida" and "U.S. credit unions."
  • Pricing pressures were noted on the lending side, especially in commercial loans.
  • Power grid issues in Puerto Rico were described as ongoing but not currently impacting economic growth.
  • Ortiz-Marcano highlighted seasonality as a factor in credit metrics but pointed to improving loan vintages as mitigating risk.

Final Takeaway

OFG Bancorp showcased record assets and robust loan origination in Q2 2025, underpinned by strong digital adoption and a strategic focus on growth, particularly in commercial lending. Management raised loan growth guidance for the year, signaling confidence in both the Puerto Rico and U.S. markets. Shareholder returns remain a priority with the announcement of a $100 million buyback, while operational efficiency and expense control continue to support the company's financial strength. The business outlook remains positive, reinforced by stable credit quality and a resilient economic environment in Puerto Rico.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Waymo doubles Austin footprint as robotaxi battle heats up
data.description:
Waymo, Alphabet’s (NASDAQ:GOOG) (GOOGL) robotaxi unit, is more than doubling its service area in Austin in a strategic push to stay ahead of Tesla (TSLA).

Waymo’s (NASDAQ:GOOG) (GOOGL) robotaxi coverage in Austin now spans 90 square miles—more than double its previous footprint—as it aims to cement its lead with what it calls the city’s only fully autonomous, 24/7 ride-hailing service, a veiled dig at Tesla (TSLA) which only offers invite-only, supervised rides between 6am and 12am within a 42 square mile range.

Through its exclusive partnership with Uber (UBER), Waymo (NASDAQ:GOOG) (GOOGL) now offers autonomous rides in new neighborhoods that include Crestview, Windsor Park, Franklin Park, Sunset Valley and The Domain and McKinney Falls State Park.

As the push into the robotaxi arena becomes more intense – Uber (UBER) recently announced a partnership with Lucid (LCID) to deploy autonomous robotaxis through the app – Tesla (TSLA) is playing catch-up in the robotaxi race, aiming for “hundreds of thousands if not millions” on the road by the end of 2026, while Waymo (GOOG) (GOOGL) already has 1,500 autonomous vehicles active across four major U.S. cities.

But while Waymo (GOOG) (GOOGL) may have the advantage of a head start, Tesla (TSLA) enjoys the cost savings of vertical integration, allowing the company to scale more quickly and efficiently, representing a cost savings of 30% to 40% per mile versus Waymo (GOOG) (GOOGL).

Ultimately, the winner may come down to who can scale faster without compromising safety and user experience, something both Waymo (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Tesla (TSLA) are racing to prove in real time.

data.title: U.S. Bancorp signals positive operating leverage of 200 bps or more amid balance sheet repositioning and fee growth focus
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: U.S. Bancorp (USB) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Gunjan Kedia, President and CEO, reported earnings per share of $1.11 on net income of $1.8 billion, highlighting "core growth across our diversified fee income businesses and continued expense discipline more than offset a lighter spread income." Kedia noted 13% year-over-year EPS growth as adjusted and "total fee revenue growth of 4.6% year-over-year reflected broad-based strength across our businesses and ongoing focus on execution and organic growth."
  • Kedia stated that U.S. Bancorp delivered 250 basis points of year-over-year positive operating leverage, marking the fourth consecutive quarter of revenue growth outpacing expense growth, and emphasized an 18% return on tangible common equity and a high-50s efficiency ratio.
  • Kedia explained, "fee income now represents approximately 42% of total net revenue. We saw good sequential growth in total purchase volumes within Payment Services, and a Fortune 500 ranking improved from a year ago."
  • Kedia detailed a balance sheet shift: "At quarter end, C&I and credit card portfolios represented 47% of the balance sheet. This is up from 43% at the end of 2023. This quarter, these average loans grew 6.6% year-over-year, vastly outpacing total loan growth."
  • Kedia announced the divestiture of approximately $6 billion in mortgage and auto loans "to strategically reposition the balance sheet, both for stronger growth and in support of deeper client relationships."
  • John C. Stern, CFO, stated, "We reported earnings per share of $1.11 and generated $7 billion of net revenue on flat expenses. Ending assets of $686 billion were impacted by seasonally elevated quarter end deposit flows. Credit quality metrics remained stable."

Outlook

  • Stern provided third quarter 2025 guidance: "We expect net interest income for the third quarter on a fully taxable equivalent basis to be in the range of $4.1 billion to $4.2 billion. Total fee revenue is expected to be approximately $3 billion. Total noninterest expense is expected to be $4.2 billion or lower in the third quarter."
  • Full year 2025 guidance remains consistent: "Compared to full year 2024, we expect total net revenue growth on an adjusted basis at the lower end of our 3% to 5% range."
  • Stern emphasized, "For the full year, we expect to deliver positive operating leverage of 200 basis points or more on an adjusted basis."

Financial Results

  • Net interest income on a fully taxable equivalent basis was $4.08 billion for the quarter.
  • Total fee revenue excluding security losses was approximately $3 billion, up 4.6% year-over-year.
  • Noninterest expense was $4.18 billion.
  • Average loans totaled $379 billion, impacted by the sale of $4.5 billion of residential mortgages and $1 billion of auto loans. Excluding these sales, average loan growth was approximately 0.4% sequentially and 1.6% year-over-year.
  • Stern noted, "average consumer deposit balances increased $2.4 billion or 1.1% linked quarter, while the percentage of noninterest-bearing to total deposits remained stable at approximately 16%."
  • The CET1 capital level was 10.7% as of June 30.

Q&A

  • Robert Scott Siefers, Piper Sandler: Asked about net interest margin and its trajectory. Stern responded, "we expect sequential net interest income growth here in the third and fourth quarter as we move forward," and highlighted strategic actions supporting margin improvement.
  • Siefers asked about expenses and investment. Stern replied, "We continued our investment in terms of our tech spend, in terms of our investments in the business. That has not changed."
  • Steven A. Alexopoulos, TD Cowen: Questioned operating leverage targets and revenue growth. Kedia stated, "200 basis points of positive operating leverage is very healthy in the long term. The opportunities and revenue growth... are very, very real."
  • Betsy Lynn Graseck, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about drivers of C&I loan growth. Stern said, "the growth really in C&I was everywhere... utilization rate did tick up a little bit."
  • Michael Mayo, Wells Fargo: Asked about deposit competition and asset-liability management. Stern acknowledged, "the deposit market is competitive. It has been for some time."
  • Erika Najarian, UBS: Questioned focus on expenses versus growth. Kedia responded, "My focus on the expenses is entirely short term because it's the fuel that helps create positive operating leverage and also helps us invest in our growth businesses."
  • John G. Pancari, Evercore ISI: Asked about loan and deposit growth outlook. Stern noted better positioning on loan growth than at the year's start.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about net interest income growth, deposit costs, and the sustainability of positive operating leverage, with a slightly negative and skeptical tone, particularly on the NII outlook.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently using phrases like "we feel very good about the trajectory" and "we are very confident" regarding strategic initiatives and financial targets.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' skepticism around NII and margin sustainability intensified, while management's tone remained consistently confident, emphasizing recent strategic actions and ongoing investments.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance remains focused on positive operating leverage of 200 basis points or more, with management reiterating its commitment to organic growth and fee income expansion.
  • The current quarter saw explicit action on balance sheet repositioning via loan divestitures, compared to last quarter's emphasis on expense discipline and early-stage organic growth.
  • Analysts in the current quarter pressed harder on NII concerns and funding costs, reflecting heightened skepticism versus the previous quarter.
  • Key metrics, such as fee revenue growth and efficiency ratio, saw improvement, while NII and margin dynamics drew increased scrutiny.
  • Management's confidence and strategic priorities (expense discipline, organic growth, payments transformation) were consistent with the previous quarter, but with greater urgency around balance sheet mix and digital investments.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged deposit pricing pressures and a competitive deposit environment.
  • Strategic loan and securities sales resulted in one-time losses ($57 million loss on securities restructuring), but were taken to optimize the balance sheet for future growth.
  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of NII growth and whether expense discipline could constrain future investments.
  • No liquidity concerns were identified by management regarding deposit trends or loan sales.

Final Takeaway

U.S. Bancorp’s second quarter results underscore a strategic pivot toward higher fee income, disciplined expense management, and an active balance sheet repositioning aimed at supporting multiservice clients and margin improvement. The company remains focused on delivering positive operating leverage, with management reiterating confidence in sustainable EPS growth and progress toward medium-term financial targets, despite persistent analyst skepticism about net interest income and deposit mix challenges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Amazon cutting jobs at AWS cloud unit
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) is cutting jobs at its cloud unit Amazon Web Services, a spokesperson for the company confirmed in an email to Seeking Alpha.

"After a thorough review of our organization, our priorities, and what we need to focus on going forward, we’ve made the difficult business decision to eliminate some roles across particular teams in AWS," said Amazon spokesperson Brad Glasser.

Amazon did not provide an exact number of people who would be impacted by the layoffs.

"These decisions are necessary as we continue to invest, hire, and optimize resources to deliver innovation for our customers,” said Glasser. He added that the company was committed to supporting the employees throughout their transition.

Amazon noted that while it was eliminating roles in parts of these teams, it was also hiring for priorities in other areas. The company is also trying to find internal opportunities for employees whose roles are impacted.

The U.S. tech giant is continuing to hire in core areas of its business and the changes were primarily about prioritization and investing in key strategic areas, according to the company.

The company added that the role reductions are not mainly because of AI and are more related to a review it did on where it thought it could be more streamlined given the results and current priorities.

Employees in the U.S. will receive their pay and benefits for at least the next 60 days, outplacement job search support, access to transitional health benefits, and be eligible for a severance payment. Support in other countries varies, as per the company.

The company added that it was following local processes and regulations, including consultation processes, in countries where applicable.

data.title: Lithium prices, stocks rise as big Chinese miner halts production
data.description:
Lithium prices jumped 4% to three-month highs on Thursday after China's Zangge Mining said it suspended lithium production at its facility in Qinghai province following directives from local government officials.

Albemarle (NYSE:ALB) +6.9% in Thursday's trading, racing to the top of the S&P 500 leaderboard; among other lithium stocks, Sigma Lithium (NASDAQ:SGML) +10%, SQM (SQM) +6.8%, Lithium Americas (LAC) +4.7%, Piedmont Lithium (PLL) +1.1%.

Zangge, which is majority owned by Zijin Mining (OTCPK:ZIJMF) (OTCPK:ZIJMY), received a notice from Haixi prefecture authorities demanding an immediate stop to non-compliant mining activities, according to the report, citing a Shenzhen exchange filing that did not disclose the specific reasons for non-compliance.

Production, which was forecast to hit 11K metric tons of lithium carbonate this year, can only resume with local government approval.

"Since it is not yet possible to determine the specific time of Zangge Lithium's resumption of production, the impact on the company cannot be predicted for the time being," the miner said.

The uncertainty has contributed to volatility in the lithium market, lifting the most actively traded lithium carbonate futures contract on the Guangzhou futures exchange to nearly a three-month high following news of the production halt.

ETF: (NYSEARCA:LIT)
